name: PHYS_MEM_MAP_MmMapIoSpace_caller_controlled
reference_drivers: RTCore64.sys (sha256 anchored, MSI Afterburner CVE-2019-16098 family)
notes: Detects MmMapIoSpace use with caller-controlled PhysicalAddress/Length where the returned mapped VA is NULL-checked and stored back to a struct/output buffer (the classic BYOVD physmem-mapper pattern). Sequence (1) catches xor r8d,r8d (CacheType=MmNonCached=0) immediately before the indirect IAT call. Sequences (2)-(5) catch post-call mov-rax-to-saved-reg + NULL-check + store, the canonical idiom whose presence + a positive_strings hit on '\Device\PhysicalMemory' or 'Physical Memory' distinguishes weaponizable BYOVD physmem mappers from legitimate storage / chipset drivers (iaStorAC, iaStorV, nvraid, vmci, ApsX64 — all match the post-call idiom but lack the PhysicalMemory string and so are filtered). RTCore64 TP=2 (1 pre + 1 post). AsrDrv10 TP=4 (3 post variants across 4 dispatcher branches). HIGH confidence is contingent on positive_strings match; without it the matcher should downgrade to MEDIUM per engine semantics. The patterns are MSVC-x64 canonical; Clang and GCC kernel toolchains may emit slightly different register selections — re-evaluate on Clang-built drivers (rare in BYOVD corpus).
